meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Finish Your First Project as a Student
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to add structure, focus, and career-defining value to your student coding portfolio today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope constraints. By defining a SaaS pricing model early, you force yourself to prioritize core features over scope creep. This roadmap uses Antigravity and Claude Code to build a functional, monetizable MVP that turns your code into a professional asset.
content.intro:
You have a graveyard of half-finished repositories. The excitement of the initial commit fades into the reality of infinite features and zero direction. You’re not failing because you lack talent; you’re failing because you lack a business constraint. Without a pricing model, your project has no 'definition of done,' leading to the endless cycle of tinkering.

Today, we pivot. By treating your student project like a real SaaS product with a defined pricing tier, you gain the clarity needed to ship. We will leverage Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, transforming your 'tutorial project' into a portfolio piece that commands attention from recruiters.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify the specific problem your SaaS solves and map it to a pricing tier. This creates a functional boundary for your development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and propose a three-tier pricing model (Free, Pro, Enterprise) based on feature access and usage limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a pricing_schema.json file based on the proposed tiers'

steps.title: Architect the Auth-to-Payment Flow
steps.description: Building the bridge between user identity and access control is where most developers stall. Simplify this by locking features behind a user status check.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean middleware pattern for checking user subscription status before allowing API access to premium endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement subscription middleware in the auth service'

steps.title: Automate Database Schemas
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your pricing tiers directly to your database models to ensure your data structure supports your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema that links User models to Subscription tiers and tracks usage quotas.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update schema.prisma to include subscription fields'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Components
steps.description: Don't over-engineer. Build a high-converting pricing table that communicates value clearly, using your predefined tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive React/Tailwind pricing component that highlights the 'Pro' tier as the best value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add PricingTable.tsx to the landing page components'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your logic to a payment gateway. This is the final step that turns a 'project' into a 'product'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler to update user subscription status in the database upon successful checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create stripe_webhook_controller.ts and test the logic'

pro_tips: Always build the paywall first; if you can't justify the price, you shouldn't be building the feature.
Use mock data for your payment gateway during development to avoid constant Stripe dashboard context switching.
Limit your 'Free' tier to a single, high-value core action to maximize user conversion.
Never build custom billing logic; always integrate with Stripe or LemonSqueezy to save 100+ hours of development time.
Document your pricing logic in your README; recruiters value business acumen as much as clean code.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, deploy your MVP to Vercel and share the link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of your pricing strategy to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished code collect digital dust. By applying a pricing model, you've turned a hobby into a product, and a student into a founder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
